retry_reason: Put wrapper: releasing a reference; retry would cause double-release (use-after-free).
contract_summary: This is a release (put) wrapper called during credential cleanup. It decrements the reference count on the AppArmor label attached to the cred via aa_put_label, and then sets the label pointer to NULL to prevent further use. The caller is the cred_free hook, which must ensure that the cred is no longer live after this call. The operation is unconditional and always releases the reference; there is no return value to check. Calling this function twice on the same cred would be a bug (double-put).

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to release a reference on the AppArmor label stored in the cred (aa_put_label) and nullify the pointer. No other business logic.

check.reason: The function calls aa_put_label(cred_label(cred)), which is a put operation on the label obtained from cred->security. The access path correctly starts from the parameter cred, traverses through security (to the aa_label pointer), then follows the callee-defined path count.count.refcount. The type chain matches the expected structure hierarchy. The segment count (5) equals the number of types in the chain (5). No refcount operation acts on a different object; the label is a member of the cred structure (via the security field).
